stories.headline: US and Iran Appear Far From Peace Deal 100 Days Since War Began
stories.summary: The US and Iran appear to be making little progress toward an interim deal to end the war Washington and Israel began 100 days ago, as fresh attacks pile pressure on a fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: The accomplishments of 100 days of war on Iran are undeniable
stories.summary: The US has not just decimated Iran's nuclear programme and military, it has also degraded the power of its regime.

stories.headline: Iran war day 100: US, Iran trade attacks again, raising tensions
stories.summary: Tensions rise as US shoots down Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz, vital for international maritime traffic.

stories.headline: Hegseth attacks Europe over migration with beach 'invasion' D-Day speech
stories.summary: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

stories.headline: Iran after 100 days of war: The triumph of survival
stories.summary: Tehran sees its ability to preserve its governing system amid a regime-change war as a clear victory.

stories.headline: US Defence Secretary Hegseth warns of 'invasion' on European shores in D-Day speech
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech in Normandy to warn European leaders about migration, saying that “boats and men” arriving on southern European shores posed a challenge that governments could no longer ignore.

stories.headline: US defence chief uses D-Day speech to warn Europe on migration ‘invasion’
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth drew controversy for saying, "Boats and men arrive. When will European capitals do something about that invasion? Or is it too late?"

stories.headline: European leaders to meet Zelenskyy at Downing Street as pressure mounts on Russia
stories.summary: The talks will focus on coordinating European support for Ukraine, increasing pressure on Russia and efforts to achieve a just and lasting peace, according to French President Emmanuel Macron.

stories.headline: How have countries around the world responded to the US-Israel war on Iran?
stories.summary: The war, which has entered its 100th day, has triggered global energy crisis, drawing concerns from world leaders.

stories.headline: How many times were the US and Iran on the verge of a deal?
stories.summary: One hundred days into the US-Israel war on Iran, we look at the number of times a deal seemed close.

stories.headline: More than 1.5 million people die and 860 million fall ill from unsafe food, the WHO warns

stories.headline: Petronas Seeks Access to Turkmenistan Gas Field, Bernama Says
stories.summary: Malaysia’s national petroleum company Petroliam Nasional Bhd is seeking access to a major gas field in Turkmenistan, Bernama reported, citing Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim.

stories.headline: More than a million people join Pope Leo for outdoor mass in Madrid

stories.headline: Kosovo votes again amid political deadlock, seeking EU and NATO progress
stories.summary: Deadlock hampers Kosovo’s EU ambitions as voters head to polls amid economic struggles and political discord.

stories.headline: Why is Chinese President Xi Jinping visiting North Korea now?
stories.summary: The Chinese leader is making his first trip to Pyongyang in seven years, amid significant military developments.

stories.headline: Protests grow in Albania against Trump-Kushner-linked resort

stories.headline: South Korea Unveils Measures to Stem Won Slide, Curb Speculation
stories.summary: South Korea laid out a series of targeted measures to curb pressure on the won after the currency slid to its weakest level since 2009, pledging firm action against speculative trading and other activities that authorities said had amplified recent market swings.

stories.headline: A Stock Trader’s Guide to the Start of ECB Interest Rate Hikes
stories.summary: European stock traders will need to add a further variable to their investment strategies this week: how rising interest rates will affect each corner of the market.

stories.headline: China’s PBOC Adds Gold Again as Bullion Remains Under Pressure
stories.summary: China’s central bank extended its gold-buying streak in May, adding to holdings as prices of the precious metal remained under pressure.

stories.headline: World’s Hottest Market Has Korea Bulls Reaching for Protection
stories.summary: A wave of optimism over South Korean stocks is giving way to growing caution, as some investors hedge positions and pare back crowded trades on concerns that the rally has run too hot, too fast.

stories.headline: RFK Jr. Appears Disengaged on Many Health Department Matters Beyond Vaccines
stories.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has demonstrated little interest in managing his sprawling department as he focuses on food and vaccine policies, according to colleagues.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: Iran says staff blocked from entering US after players given World Cup visas

stories.headline: ECB Steps Up as G7’s Lead Hawk With Interest-Rate Hike Primed
stories.summary: A euro-zone interest-rate hike in the coming week is set to place the European Central Bank at the vanguard of global tightening caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Fed’s Barr Warns of Risks Tied to Looser Wall Street Bank Rules
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr criticized moves from regulators over the past year to relax the rules for US lenders, saying the proposals “considerably weaken bank regulation and supervision.”

stories.headline: Bob Packwood, Senator Forced to Quit in Sex Scandal, Dies at 93
stories.summary: An unorthodox Republican, he helped shape tax policy and women’s rights legislation before resigning in 1995 amid accusations by more than 20 women of sexual misconduct.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on

stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long, CNN Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppman, Fmr. US Ambassador to Israel Tom Nides and Grindr CEO George Arison. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: C.I.A. Officer Found With Gold Bars Said to Have Created Fake Spy Program
stories.summary: A federal judge has ruled that David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, must remain in detention. U.S. officials say Mr. Rush funneled millions in federal funds to himself.

stories.headline: Man dies after shark attack off Western Australia coast near Albany
stories.summary: Government department says man was in the water around Michaelmas Island, near Albany, when he was bitten by a suspected 4.5-metre sharkGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA male diver aged in his 30s has died after being bitten by a shark in Western Australia.The state’s police force confirmed on Saturday afternoon that the 35-year-old man had died, after being treated by paramedics at the scene for more than two hours. stories.headline: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
stories.summary: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rainTyphoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders.

stories.headline: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
stories.summary: A committee of MPs has been formed to look at the case against Cyril Ramaphosa, but he may survive a vote.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.summary: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plasticsTakeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis.The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and

stories.headline: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
stories.summary: Demonstrators say the project backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law would harm a protected environment.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Miracle on Everest: Guide believed dead spotted crawling down ice
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.headline: Tough cookies: How pop group Le Sserafim overcame band tensions and internet trolls
stories.summary: The K-pop band say accepting their flaws and embracing humour took them to a new level of success.

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero charged with assault

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
stories.summary: Teachers demanding higher pay have been demonstrating for several days and threaten to disrupt the tournament if their demands are not met.

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
stories.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico

stories.headline: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
